title: Should Massachusetts stop changing its clocks?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers are debating a bill to end the practice of changing clocks twice a year.
Senate President Karen E. Spilka supports legislation S.2157, which proposes permanent time to reduce disruptions.
Research links time changes to health issues, prompting discussions on switching to Atlantic Standard Time year-round.

title: Boston Pride For The People announces 2026 parade and festival date
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Boston Pride For The People announces the 2026 Pride celebration for June 6, featuring a parade and festival.
The parade starts at 11 a.m. at Copley Square and ends at Boston Common with additional events planned.
Mayor Michelle Wu praises the event's focus on diversity and inclusivity, welcoming attendees from across the region.

title: Feds release teen detained by ICE in Marlborough
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A 14-year-old Brazilian girl detained by ICE in Marlborough was released and returned to Massachusetts by federal officials.
Federal agents allegedly detained her to find her father, but DHS claims she was rescued from gang members.
Democratic lawmakers criticized the handling of the case, and the girl faces a court appearance that could lead to deportation.

title: With ‘top guys’ struggling, a lineup reshuffle might be needed for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: David Pastrnak and Elias Lindholm have struggled in 5-on-5 play, prompting lineup considerations for the Boston Bruins.
The Bruins' power play has declined, converting only 4 out of 28 attempts in the last eight games.
Coach Marco Sturm is considering lineup changes as the team holds a narrow playoff spot lead in the Eastern Conference.

title: Swiss student came to Boston to study rehab. Now he’s running for trauma survivors.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Alexis Cogne, 26, a visiting researcher at Harvard Medical School, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for trauma survivors.
Originally from Geneva, Switzerland, Cogne aims to raise $10,000 for the Stepping Strong Center at Brigham and Women's Hospital.
His work focuses on neurorehabilitation, emphasizing the courage required for recovery from severe injuries and the need for research funding.

title: Fire destroys Newbury home, kills pets as family escapes
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Fire destroyed a home at 49 Fruit St., Newbury; residents escaped uninjured, but a dog and cat perished.
Newbury Fire Department responded before 1 a.m. with mutual aid from nearby towns; home was fully engulfed.
Fire spread to vehicles and gas grills; residents found temporary lodging; investigation into the cause is ongoing.

title: How a Cambridge home cook built a 1,000-member recipe club around local ingredients
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Andrew Little founded the Cambridge Recipe Club to focus on local ingredients and community connection.
The club has grown to over 1,000 members who receive weekly recipes using Massachusetts produce.
Little collaborates with club members and local chefs, sharing both beginner-friendly and diverse recipes.

title: Campbell to continue suit against Live Nation, as internal messages show employee mocked customers
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Massachusetts AG Andrea Campbell vows to continue antitrust litigation against Live Nation despite DOJ settlement.
Internal Slack messages from Live Nation's employee mocking customers have been made public, sparking scrutiny.
Live Nation operates over 265 venues in North America; Ticketmaster handles 80% of primary ticketing, says Campbell's office.

title: Healey, Campbell launch portal for reporting ICE misconduct
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Governor Maura Healey and Attorney General Andrea Campbell launched a portal for reporting ICE misconduct in Massachusetts.
The portal collects written reports, photos, and videos to identify misconduct patterns and inform legal actions or policy.
Campbell's office also released an updated 'know your rights' guide for immigrants regarding ICE arrests and federal accountability.

title: Jaylen Brown criticizes NBA officials for allowing foul-baiting, rewarding flopping
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown criticized NBA officials for rewarding flopping and foul-baiting after Celtics' loss to Oklahoma City.
Brown expressed frustration over not receiving calls for strong drives, suggesting flopping might be necessary to sell fouls.
Despite his criticism, Brown scored 34 points and played 41 minutes, making 13 of 14 free throws against Oklahoma City.
